display.purpose: Page titles and major workspace headings.

title.purpose: Board cards, note cards, and item names.

label.purpose: Section labels, counts, compact metadata, and filters.

shadows.name: card-elevation
shadows.purpose: Default scannable elevation for workspace cards.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Default color, border, and shadow state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default create, save, and confirm action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { height: 40px; padding: 0 20px; border: 0; border-radius: 2rem; background: #111111; color: #F5F3EE; font: 700 14px/1 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease, transform 200ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1A1A1A; transform: scale(1.03); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #111111; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { height: 40px; padding: 0 20px; border: 2px solid rgba(17,17,17,0.2); border-radius: 2rem; background: #E8E4DD; color: #111111; font: 700 14px/1 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 180ms ease, transform 200ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #111111; transform: scale(1.03); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #111111; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Rounded tactile field for search, rename, and form entry.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 40px; width: 260px; border: 2px solid rgba(17,17,17,0.3); border-radius: 16px; background: transparent; color: #111111; padding: 0 12px; font: 400 14px/1.4 Space Mono, ui-monospace, monospace; transition: border-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: rgba(17,17,17,0.4); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #111111; }

components.name: Workspace Card
components.description: Scannable card for plans, notes, and workspace objects.
components.css: .ds-workspace-card { display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: flex-start; padding: 20px; border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08); background: #E8E4DD; color: #111111; box-shadow: rgba(0,0,0,0.04) 0 4px 18px, rgba(0,0,0,0.027) 0 2.025px 7.84688px, rgba(0,0,0,0.02) 0 0.8px 2.925px, rgba(0,0,0,0.01) 0 0.175px 1.04062px; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-workspace-card:hover { border-color: rgba(0,0,0,0.14); box-shadow: rgba(0,0,0,0.06) 0 6px 22px, rgba(0,0,0,0.04) 0 3px 10px, rgba(0,0,0,0.03) 0 1px 4px, rgba(0,0,0,0.02) 0 0.25px 1.5px; } .ds-card-icon { width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 10px; display: grid; place-items: center; background: rgba(230,59,46,0.12); color: #E63B2E; font: 700 14px/1 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-workspace-card h3 { margin: 0 0 6px; font: 700 22px/1.15 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-workspace-card p { margin: 0; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.35); font: 500 12px/1.3 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: Sidebar Item
components.description: Compact navigation row for plans, notes, and drawings.
components.css: .ds-sidebar-item { width: 240px; height: 36px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; border: 0; border-radius: 12px; background: transparent; color: rgba(245,243,238,0.6); padding: 0 12px; text-align: left; font: 500 14px/1 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms ease, color 180ms ease; } .ds-sidebar-item:hover { background: rgba(245,243,238,0.08); color: #F5F3EE; } .ds-sidebar-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F5F3EE; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-sidebar-dot { width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(230,59,46,0.2); }

narrative.overview:
PlanThing should feel like a quiet workbench where plans can grow from rough notes into organized action. The interface is calm and clean first, then tactile and fast in the hand: boards, cards, notes, and drawing surfaces should feel direct, not precious.

The baseline borrows Notion's clarity and warm restraint, but the product must never read as a generic Notion clone. PlanThing's identity comes from planning, growth, and adaptable structure: a warm paper field, dark anchored navigation, red sprout-like accent, and controls that feel sturdy enough for daily use.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper backgrounds instead of sterile white or cold gray.
Dark, grounded sidebar for navigation and workspace memory.
Red accent used for brand punctuation, danger, and small moments of energy.
Rounded tactile controls with clear hover and focus states.
Dense product surfaces that stay calm through hierarchy, not decoration.
Motion is quick feedback only, with reduced-motion support.

rules.name: The Red Is Alive Rule
rules.body: Red is punctuation, not wallpaper. If multiple red elements compete on one screen, the screen is shouting.

rules.name: The Paper Before Chrome Rule
rules.body: Default to warm paper surfaces and border contrast before adding shadows, gradients, or decorative effects.

rules.name: The Product Type Rule
rules.body: UI labels, data, and controls use Inter or Space Mono. Serif italic is a brand accent, never the default language of the interface.

rules.name: The Flat Until Useful Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat by default. Elevation appears when it helps scanning, interaction, or temporary layering.

dos: Do keep PlanThing calm and clean even when plans, notes, and drawings grow dense.
Do use Paper Field, Paper Panel, and Ink Black as the main structural palette.
Do make customization visible through board colors, icon wells, labels, views, and flexible workspace organization.
Do provide visible focus states, reduced-motion alternatives, color-blind safe labels, and keyboard-first workflows for core actions.
Do use Notion as a clarity baseline while giving PlanThing its own planning and growth identity.
donts: Don't make PlanThing feel like corporate Jira.
Don't make PlanThing feel like a generic Notion clone.
Don't use neon AI dashboard styling, glowing dark gradients, or decorative AI visual tropes.
Don't make the interface feel like a childish task app.
Don't let boards become a cluttered Trello board without hierarchy.
Don't use over-designed startup SaaS hero patterns inside product surfaces.
Don't rely on color alone for labels, statuses, selection, errors, or permissions.
